description: MCP Tasks Extension Schema. Contains only task-related definitions from the MCP 2025-11-25 specification. Core MCP types are referenced via remote $ref pointing to the 2025-11-25 core schema.

TaskMetadata.description:
Metadata for augmenting a request with task execution.
Include this in the `task` field of the request parameters.

ttl.description: Requested duration in milliseconds to retain task from creation.

RelatedTaskMetadata.description:
Metadata for associating messages with a task.
Include this in the `_meta` field under the key `io.modelcontextprotocol/related-task`.

taskId.description: The task identifier this message is associated with.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the task was created.

lastUpdatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the task was last updated.

statusMessage.description:
Optional human-readable message describing the current task state.
This can provide context for any status, including:
- Reasons for "cancelled" status
- Summaries for "completed" status
- Diagnostic information for "failed" status (e.g., error details, what went wrong)

ttl.description: Actual retention duration from creation in milliseconds, null for unlimited.

CreateTaskResult.description: A response to a task-augmented request.

_meta.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

TaskAugmentedRequestParams.description: Common params for any task-augmented request.

_meta.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

progressToken.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by notifications/progress). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.

task.description:
If specified, the caller is requesting task-augmented execution for this request.
The request will return a CreateTaskResult immediately, and the actual result can be
retrieved later via tasks/result.

Task augmentation is subject to capability negotiation - receivers MUST declare support
for task augmentation of specific request types in their capabilities.

GetTaskRequest.description: A request to retrieve the state of a task.

GetTaskResult.description: The response to a tasks/get request.

GetTaskPayloadRequest.description: A request to retrieve the result of a completed task.

taskId.description: The task identifier to retrieve results for.

GetTaskPayloadResult.description:
The response to a tasks/result request.
The structure matches the result type of the original request.
For example, a tools/call task would return the CallToolResult structure.

_meta.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

CancelTaskRequest.description: A request to cancel a task.

CancelTaskResult.description: The response to a tasks/cancel request.

ListTasksRequest.description: A request to retrieve a list of tasks.

ListTasksResult.description: The response to a tasks/list request.

_meta.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

nextCursor.description:
An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
If present, there may be more results available.

TaskStatusNotification.description: An optional notification from the receiver to the requestor, informing them that a task's status has changed. Receivers are not required to send these notifications.

TaskServerCapabilities.description: Task-related server capabilities. Include this in the server's capabilities object during initialization.

call.description: Whether the server supports task-augmented tools/call requests.

TaskClientCapabilities.description: Task-related client capabilities. Include this in the client's capabilities object during initialization.

createMessage.description: Whether the client supports task-augmented sampling/createMessage requests.

create.description: Whether the client supports task-augmented elicitation/create requests.

ToolTaskSupport.description:
Task support declaration for individual tools.
- "forbidden": Tool does not support task-augmented execution (default when absent)
- "optional": Tool may support task-augmented execution
- "required": Tool requires task-augmented execution
